summary: AIR-DROW On-device Launch Validation & Final Release Gate: a private preflight now checks the canvas, PNG/JPG encoding and local file preparation without opening the camera, downloading a file, inspecting artwork or sending data.
features: Adds a browser-local launch-validation probe for the drawing surface, PNG/JPG encoding and local object-URL preparation.
Keeps real downloads, exports, shares, backups and destructive clear actions behind trusted physical controls only.
Keeps camera permission and live hand validation explicitly user initiated; the readiness check never calls getUserMedia.
Adds source QA and a bilingual release note that distinguishes internal browser checks from real Android camera/download validation.
Synchronizes page, runtime, PWA manifest, service-worker cache and generated deployment output under v8.1.0 revision 810.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you choose to export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: App readiness, on-device launch validation, hand drawing checks and local diagnostics read only browser/app state. They do not open the camera, trigger a download, upload a report, or include camera frames, hand landmarks, drawing points, project titles, or API URLs.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and hand model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The complete source package includes the verified hand model. The production build validates the local model before publishing.
